report_name: Qilin Ransomware Attacks German Party Die Linke, Threatens Data Leak

threat_actors.name: Qilin
threat_actors.description: A Russia-speaking Ransomware-as-a-Service (RaaS) group that claimed responsibility for a cyberattack against the German political party Die Linke. The group uses double-extortion tactics, threatening to release stolen data on its dark web leak site.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion of the victim organization through data encryption and the threat of leaking stolen sensitive information.

threat_actors.name: APT29
threat_actors.description: Russian state-sponsored group mentioned for context, as their previous campaigns against German political entities (like the Christian Democratic Union) are seen as part of a broader hybrid warfare context in which the Qilin attack is viewed by the victim.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: Qilin ransomware encrypts a victim's files as a primary component of its extortion strategy.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: The Qilin group exfiltrates sensitive data from victims before encryption to use as leverage in their double-extortion tactics.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: Cited as a typical initial access vector for Qilin affiliates to compromise victim networks.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: Mentioned as a common initial access method used in Qilin ransomware attacks.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: Qilin's playbook includes deleting volume shadow copies using vssadmin to hinder system restoration from local backups.

malware.name: Qilin
malware.description: The ransomware payload deployed by the Qilin group to encrypt files on victim networks and facilitate a double-extortion scheme.

tools.name: vssadmin
tools.description: The vssadmin.exe utility is used by ransomware groups like Qilin to delete volume shadow copies, preventing system recovery.

identities.name: Die Linke
identities.description: A German democratic socialist political party that was targeted by the Qilin ransomware group in March 2026.

identities.name: Christian Democratic Union
identities.description: A German political party mentioned as a past target of APT29, providing context for cyberattacks against political entities in Germany.

infrastructure.name: Qilin Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A dark web site used by the Qilin ransomware group to threaten victims and publish stolen internal documents and employee data if the ransom is not paid.

indicators.name: Qilin Ransom Note File Creation
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Qilin Ransom Note File Creation
id: 2b1a9c8d-4f5e-4b6c-8d7a-1b2c3d4e5f6a
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of the Qilin ransom note file 'How To Restore Your Files.txt'.
references:
  - https://therecord.media/die-linke-german-political-party-qilin-ransomware
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-06
tags:
  - attack.t1486
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '\How To Restore Your Files.txt'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation of the ransom note file named 'How To Restore Your Files.txt', which is associated with Qilin ransomware attacks.

indicators.name: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
id: f8a1b2c3-d4e5-f6a7-b8c9-d0e1f2a3b4c5
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common ransomware tactic to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://therecord.media/die-linke-german-political-party-qilin-ransomware
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-06
tags:
  - attack.t1490
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of 'vssadmin.exe' with commands to delete volume shadow copies, a technique used by ransomware like Qilin to prevent recovery.
